title: Mappers
description:
The Mappers directory is responsible for processing input data and transforming it into a format suitable for further processing by the reducers. It contains classes that implement the map() method of the MapReduce framework, which is called for each input record.

The RegexMapper class, for example, extracts text matching a regular expression pattern from the input data. It uses the Configuration class to set up the regular expression pattern and group, and emits Text keys and LongWritable values as the output.

The classes in this directory collaborate by passing data to each other through the map() method's parameters and return values. They may also use shared resources or communicate with other components of the system as needed.

The architectural role of this directory is to define the data processing logic and transform the input data into a format that is suitable for the reducers to process. This involves defining the input and output formats, as well as the processing logic for each input record.

The directory follows the MapReduce pattern, which is a common approach for processing large-scale data using distributed computing. It divides the data processing into two main phases: the map phase and the reduce phase. The mappers in this directory are responsible for the map phase, which involves transforming the input data into a format suitable for the reducers.
